desc: Kraków pairs a beautifully preserved medieval core with major museums, café culture, and easy excursions, making it one of Central Europe's most complete city breaks.
hls: UNESCO-listed Old Town and Wawel setting
Deep history from medieval to modern eras
Good transport for Wieliczka/Auschwitz day trips
Christmas markets and festive winter appeal

todo.name: Old Town & Wawel loop
todo.desc: Walk from Rynek Główny to Wawel Castle and cathedral to anchor your first day in Kraków.

todo.name: Kazimierz neighborhoods
todo.desc: Explore synagogues, street art, and independent cafés in Kraków's historic Jewish quarter.

todo.name: Cloth Hall + museum picks
todo.desc: Mix main-square landmarks with one focused museum visit (e.g., Czartoryski or MOCAK).

todo.name: Vistula riverside evening
todo.desc: Use the boulevards and bars for an easy sunset walk in warmer months.

todo.name: Wieliczka Salt Mine
todo.desc: Half-day underground chambers and carvings; reserve ahead in peak season.

todo.name: Seasonal market crawl
todo.desc: Target Easter fairs, summer courtyards, or the strong Advent/Christmas market period.

wifi.note: Excellent in central stays, coworking spaces, and most cafés.

prac.fltNote: Kraków Airport is close to the center and has broad UK/EU connectivity, including frequent low-cost options.

airports.name: Kraków John Paul II International Airport

prac.visa: Poland is in Schengen; standard Schengen entry rules apply.
alerts: Old Town and main square pricing rises in peak summer and holiday weeks.

sweet: Late April–June and September–October are best for walkability; December is a strong niche pick for markets and festive ambiance.
